prompt: {{mode==1 ? 'Failure costs are eating the budget. To cut the total cost of quality, tap the category to invest MORE in to stop defects upstream.' : 'Tap the bar for the largest cost of NONconformance.'}}
caption: Prevention & appraisal = conformance; internal & external failure = nonconformance.

items.label: b[0]
items.why: mode==1 ? (b[3]==1 ? 'Prevention — spend here to stop defects before they become costly failures (the COQ leverage point).' : (b[2]==1 ? 'A failure cost — the symptom of too little prevention, not where you invest first.' : 'Appraisal finds defects but does not prevent them.')) : (b[2]==0 ? 'A conformance cost (prevention/appraisal), not a failure cost.' : (b[1]==maxFailVal ? 'A failure (nonconformance) cost, and the larger of the two.' : 'A failure cost, but smaller than the other failure bucket.'))

explanation: {{mode==1 ? 'Prevention is the cost-of-quality leverage point — a dollar spent preventing defects saves many in internal and external failure costs.' : 'Nonconformance = failure costs (internal + external). '}}{{mode==1 ? '' : worstName + ' is the larger failure bucket; prevention and appraisal are conformance costs.'}}

prompt: Every bar is within the specification limits, but the process is poorly centred. Tap the bar with the least clearance to a limit — the first output to fail if the process drifts.

items.why: i == badIdx ? ('Closest to the ' + nearName + ' with almost no margin — the first to go out of spec if the process drifts.') : 'Has comfortable clearance to both limits.'

explanation: Inside the limits isn't enough: this process is shifted toward the {{nearName}}, so the nearest bar has almost no margin. Centring the process (better Cpk) would restore clearance on both sides.
